summary: Large Big Blue stage decomp/matching PR focused on src/melee/gr/grbigblue.c, with related Big Blue headers and ground type updates. Evidence shows new implementations or major progress for multiple grBigBlue functions, two newly matched functions reported by the bot, typed Big Blue ground data additions, and review feedback on F32_MAX constants, gobj_id literal style, and simplifying empty control-flow blocks.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching body; 2599 added and 245 deleted lines across 36 hunks, including large implementations for grBigBlue_801E6C60 and grBigBlue_801E93D8, typed data references, HSD asserts, JObj calls, and stage/ground helper usage.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/gr/grbigblue.c include additions placeholder.h and baselib/debug.h
raw_diff: grBigBlue_801E6C60 replaced /// stub with large function body
raw_diff: grBigBlue_801E93D8 replaced /// stub/nearby stubs with large function body
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for main/melee/gr/grbigblue

key_files.role: Big Blue static declarations/types updated alongside the C implementation.
Ground/Big Blue type surface updated; diff excerpt shows grbigblue.c using gv.bigblue.xD4, gv.bigblue.data, and x0_w/x0_b1 fields instead of older raw offsets/older names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBigBlue_801E6904 initializes gp->gv.bigblue.xD4[] and gp->gv.bigblue.data[] fields
raw_diff: fn_801E6124 and grBigBlue_801E613C use x0_b1 instead of x0_b0

key_files.role: Public Big Blue header touched with small signature/declaration updates; exact declarations are not in the loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder/todo regions in grbigblue.c with large decompiled implementations, including grBigBlue_801E6C60 and grBigBlue_801E93D8 in the loaded excerpt.
Bot report recorded 2 new matches in main/melee/gr/grbigblue: fn_801E8560 and grBigBlue_801E8794 reached 100.00%.

Bot report recorded 10 improvements in unmatched items, including grBigBlue_801E6C60 from 0.00% to 94.47%, grBigBlue_801E93D8 from 0.00% to 90.88%, grBigBlue_801ECB50 to 91.45%, grBigBlue_801EE398 to 87.41%, grBigBlue_801EA05C to 90.07%, grBigBlue_801EBAF8 to 91.31%, .sdata2 to 87.69%, .data to 47.21%, grBigBlue_801EDF44 to 91.67%, and grBigBlue_801EACE8 to 99.80%.

Bot report also recorded regressions in unmatched items, including grBigBlue_801EB4AC, grBigBlue_801EC6C0, fn_801EF60C, .rodata, grBigBlue_801E6364, grBigBlue_801EB004, .sdata, and grBigBlue_801E8D64.
Introduced typed local structs grBb_Data803E2D78 and grBb_Data803E2EB8 and replaced tmpPadData-style pointer arithmetic in the shown grBigBlue_801E6364 excerpt with grBb_803E2D78.x48 references.
Updated Big Blue GroundVars-style access in shown code, including xD4[] JObj array and data[] entries for three Big Blue objects, replacing older individual fields/raw offsets in grBigBlue_801E6904.
Added HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT usage and baselib/debug.h include in grbigblue.c excerpts.

Review feedback identified literal/style cleanup opportunities: use F32_MAX for -3.4028235e38f sentinel, represent gobj_id values in decimal rather than hex, and simplify empty if blocks to break statements where applicable.
smart_moves: Moved from raw tmpPadData offset access toward named temporary structs and typed externs for Big Blue data tables in the loaded grBigBlue_801E6364 excerpt.

Grouped repeated Big Blue per-object state into xD4[] and data[] access patterns for the three stage objects, improving reviewability compared with separate x10/x14/x18 and raw offset writes shown in the diff.
Kept match-progress evidence visible through the bot report, which distinguishes new matches, improvements, and regressions rather than claiming a clean all-around match.

Used existing stage/ground/JObj helpers in the decompiled excerpts, including Ground_801C3FA4, grFZeroCar_801CAFBC, HSD_JObjGetTranslation, HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, Stage_GetBlastZone*Offset, and Stage_GetCamBounds*Offset.
decomp_lessons: When a magic float sentinel is actually F32_MAX, prefer the project macro/constant form over spelling out -3.4028235e38f; this is directly supported by review feedback on grBigBlue_801E6C60.
For gobj_id arguments, reviewer feedback says decimal notation is preferred over hex in this PR context.
Empty control-flow bodies generated during matching should be challenged; reviewer feedback suggested some empty blocks in grBigBlue_801E93D8 could simply be break statements.

Typed arrays/struct fields can replace raw byte-offset table access when enough local structure is recovered, as shown by replacing tmpPadData offset casts with grBb_803E2D78.x48 in grBigBlue_801E6364.
Large partial decomp PRs may legitimately improve major functions while regressing nearby functions or data sections; keep the regression ledger visible and avoid overclaiming a complete match.
naming_conventions: Big Blue stage symbols use grBigBlue_ function names for functions and grBb_ prefixes for Big Blue data/static symbols, as shown by grBigBlue_801E6C60, grBigBlue_801E93D8, grBb_804D69C8, grBb_803E2D78, and grBb_803E2EB8.

Recovered-but-not-fully-semantic struct fields in this slice use offset-like names such as x48, x58, xD4, x0_w, x1, x8, x38, and x50; the evidence supports these as current names, not final semantics.

Local variables use short role names where clear in the excerpt, such as gp for Ground*, gobj for Ground_GObj*, jobj for HSD_JObj*, pos/neg_pos for Vec3 positions, and rand_val/height_rand for random values.
Reviewer style note: represent gobj_id literals in decimal, not hex.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK appears in the loaded grBigBlue_801E93D8 excerpt, including PAD_STACK(76); no loaded evidence explains its verifier justification, so treat as a matching tactic requiring review evidence under current standards.

The diff uses a preserved base Ground* pointer in grBigBlue_801E6C60 while iterating/manipulating per-object state, aligning with the natural-loops standard's warning to keep base pointers available when later codegen needs the base address.

The loaded grBigBlue_801E6C60 excerpt uses a do loop and per-index data[] state, suggesting a natural loop shape over repeated Big Blue object state rather than copied blocks; exact final match status for this function is partial at 94.47% per bot report.
Magic sentinel comparisons to -3.4028235e38f appeared in the review hunk and were identified as F32_MAX by review; the diff excerpt shows at least some comparisons converted to -F32_MAX.

The PR uses typed field recovery and temporary local structs for data table access, but also still contains raw byte-pointer access in loaded grBigBlue_801E93D8 excerpts, so typed-field cleanup was partial in the loaded evidence.

follow_up_queries: Find final merged grbigblue.c around grBigBlue_801E6C60 to see whether all -3.4028235e38f sentinel comparisons were converted to F32_MAX.
Search grbigblue.c for Ground_801C2BA4(0x20) or other hex gobj_id literals to see whether reviewer decimal-style feedback was applied before merge.
Inspect final grBigBlue_801E93D8 switch cases for empty if blocks versus break statements after review discussion.
Search final grbigblue.c for remaining PAD_STACK usage and whether adjacent function regressions from the bot report were later resolved.
Inspect grbigblue.static.h and types.h from this PR to classify the exact Big Blue GroundVars fields added or renamed.
Search later PRs for grBb_Data803E2D78, grBb_Data803E2EB8, xD4, and data[] to see whether temporary offset-style names were refined.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use F32_MAX for Big Blue sentinel float comparisons
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2428, review on grBigBlue_801E6C60 identified the literal -3.4028235e38f as F32_MAX. For similar decomp code, prefer the existing F32_MAX constant/macro when the sentinel is the maximum float value rather than retaining the raw decimal literal.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: PsiLupan discussion_r3117959116 says 'This is F32_MAX.'
raw_diff: grBigBlue_801E6C60 compares right_y/left_y and pos.y against -3.4028235e38f and later shows -F32_MAX in comparisons

accepted_candidate_records.title: Represent gobj_id literals in decimal
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2428, reviewer feedback on grBigBlue_801E93D8 stated that a gobj_id should be represented with decimal notation rather than hex.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: PsiLupan discussion_r3117993639 says 'This is a gobj_id, we should represent it with decimal, not hex.'

accepted_candidate_records.title: Challenge empty generated control-flow blocks
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2428, reviewer feedback on grBigBlue_801E93D8 questioned empty generated-looking blocks and suggested they may simply be break statements. Treat empty if/body artifacts in switch reconstruction as suspicious and verify whether a plain break expresses the source control flow.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: PsiLupan discussion_r3118017815 says 'These are just `break;`, no?'
metadata_json.review_feedback_examples includes a follow-up noting fallthrough nuance

source_update_candidates.title: Literal style for gobj_id values
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking a standard note that gobj_id arguments/values should be written in decimal when review/local style identifies them as object IDs, rather than in hex.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides direct reviewer feedback, but one PR comment is not enough for the intake agent to promote this as a global accepted standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer named F32_MAX over raw maximum-float decimal sentinels
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a standards note or example that raw -3.4028235e38f sentinel comparisons should be written as -F32_MAX when local headers/macros support that meaning.
source_update_candidates.reason: Matches existing readability principles, but intake only has one direct PR review example and should route global-standard promotion to the curator.

source_update_candidates.title: Big Blue stage implementation path
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/gr/grbigblue.c is the main implementation file for Big Blue stage ground code, with related declarations/types in src/melee/gr/grbigblue.h, src/melee/gr/grbigblue.static.h, and src/melee/gr/types.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path relationship is strongly suggested by changed files and names, but path-fact updates belong to curator-owned sources.

source_update_candidates.text: This PR introduced temporary/recovered structs grBb_Data803E2D78 and grBb_Data803E2EB8 with fields including x48[30] and x58[4], and externs grBb_803E2D78 and grBb_803E2EB8. Curator may check whether these should be captured in data/source notes after validating final names and ownership.
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff supports that these structs were introduced, but field semantics and data ownership are not fully established by the loaded excerpt.

rejection_notes: Do not promote every offset-like field name from this PR as semantic; many names are xNN or pad_N and only show current recovery state.
Do not claim grBigBlue_801E6C60 or grBigBlue_801E93D8 matched; the bot report shows them as partial improvements, not 100%.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(76) as endorsed style; loaded evidence shows its use but not a justification or final verifier evidence.
Do not infer that bot-reported regressions were accepted or fixed later; the loaded slice does not include final resolution details.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the quoted comments, especially for the fallthrough/break discussion where the full thread is missing.
